top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Six stackable triangular stepping stones in two sizes. Each stone has three slope sides so you can dial difficulty when laying a path.
top_picks.ember_verdict: When cushions-and-tape has run its course, this is the proper path kit. Lay step–pause–turn rules on laminate without daredevil height. Each stone’s three sides progress difficulty without buying more. Pricey — borrow from nursery or try pre-loved before committing.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to follow simple movement rules like step, pause and turn rather than random crashing around
top_picks.why_it_fits: Variable steepness and two sizes create sequenced, rule-based paths — the core 34–36m shift versus earlier throw-and-aim games.
top_picks.caveats: Premium price; one set may feel short for long corridors — extend with tape markers rather than duplicating the same set.
top_picks.gift_note: Strong gift if the family has floor space and already plays active indoor games. Pair with a simple rule card.
top_picks.ownership_note: Many nurseries own these — ask before duplicating. Common on pre-loved sites.
top_picks.safety_notes: Non-slip rubber base. Supervise on hard floors until your child knows their limits — planned movement, not height daredevil play.
top_picks.rating_source: Amazon UK aggregated listing for Gonge 1137385 (same SKU)
top_picks.review_quality_note: Sustained praise for reconfigurability and floor grip; Liberty House shows verified UK owner reviews on check date.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Gonge River Stones regularly appear on Vinted and eBay UK; check rubber rim wear.
top_picks.substitute_if_unavailable: Tippi Sensory Stepping Stones 5-pack (budget path starter)
top_picks.rank_rationale: #1 over #2: three steepness settings and two stone sizes create richer rule-based paths than flat budget stones, which fits the nearly-three sequenced-movement brief better.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Three stackable hilltop platforms at different fixed heights. Non-slip feet; designed not to tip when stepped on carefully.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Pick when the shift is step-up, pause on the tall one, turn, step down — height sequencing rather than flat hopping. Combine with River Stones for a mixed path. Hands nearby until confident; this brief is planned movement, not bouncing thrills.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to practise stepping up and down with deliberate pauses rather than running at speed
top_picks.why_it_fits: Three fixed heights teach planned up-down movement — distinct from flat stones and from 31–33m throw games.
top_picks.caveats: Only three pieces — short path unless combined with cushions or River Stones. Liberty House public review count is thin.
top_picks.gift_note: Impressive for active families with cleared floor space. Best after flat stones.
top_picks.ownership_note: Pairs with River Stones — check whether family already owns Gonge before doubling up.
top_picks.safety_notes: Non-slip rubber feet; anti-overturn design. Supervise height steps until your child can pause without hands.

top_picks.ember_verdict: This is the path half of the category. Lay a walking line down the hall: heel-toe, pause at the end, turn, come back. Lower than stones so it suits cautious nearly-threes who need rule-based walking before jumping.
top_picks.personalization_hint: likely to practise careful heel-toe walking and stopping on command rather than big jumps
top_picks.why_it_fits: Beam paths train planned step-and-pause movement without jump height — distinct product type from all stone picks.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: Two box-play best friends bristle when Shu wants to join their cardboard adventures on Sudden Hill.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Friend play is getting messier now — a third child can feel like being pushed out. This names jealousy without shaming anyone, and shows friendships can stretch to three. Borrow from the library first; it turns up in nurseries a lot.
top_picks.why_it_fits: Models three-way friendship tension, exclusion and repair — the core 34–36m shift beyond emotion labelling.
top_picks.caveats: LoveReading lists “5+ readers” as a reading level — Scholastic’s 3–6 shelf mark is the age signal used here.
top_picks.gift_note: Warm after a messy playdate — not a lecture.
top_picks.ownership_note: Borrow first — common in nurseries and hand-me-down boxes.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Strong parent praise for jealousy-and-repair theme; Bookshop.org aggregate corroborates high scores.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Picture books hold up well second-hand.
top_picks.substitute_if_unavailable: Pip and Posy: The New Friend
top_picks.rank_rationale: #1 over #2: three-way jealousy and repair is the hardest nearly-three friend moment — this story centres that exact triangle rather than a two-child beach snub.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Primary set to LoveReading4Kids after Scholastic UK showed out-of-stock on check date and Bookshop.org returned intermittent 403 on availability fetch; Scholastic age mark captured separately.
top_picks.description: Two box-play best friends bristle when Shu wants to join their cardboard adventures on Sudden Hill.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: At the beach, Posy wakes to find Pip playing with new friend Zac — jealousy, then a small kind act reunites them.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Not another feelings chart — this is the moment your child watches their best friend laugh with someone else. Posy fixes it herself, which gives you language for real playgroup wobbles. Borrow if you already own other Pip & Posy titles.
top_picks.why_it_fits: Shows jealousy, inclusion and repair in peer play without adult rescue — distinct from 31–33m worry-retelling books.
top_picks.caveats: Paperback is the value pick; board book ISBN differs.
top_picks.gift_note: Warm story gift after a playdate bust-up.
top_picks.ownership_note: High duplication risk — check the shelf and nursery bag first.
top_picks.review_quality_note: Consistently high scores across Nosy Crow editions.
top_picks.preloved_signal_note: Ubiquitous second-hand — check for creased covers only.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #2 over #3: the two-child jealousy beat is more immediately recognisable at playgroup age than Sour Grape’s grudge-loop humour, and Pip & Posy has deeper UK parent recognition.
top_picks.evidence_notes: Primary switched from Nosy Crow to Amazon UK after Nosy Crow returned 403 on availability fetch while remaining smoke-OK; Nosy Crow retained as alternate.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A grape who stockpiles grudges learns forgiveness after realising a birthday mix-up — and how sour holding on feels.
top_picks.ember_verdict: When friend play goes wrong, nearly-threes can stay cross long after the toy is back. This is funny enough for repeat reads and gives you a light way into sorry and moving on. Library copy is fine if you already own The Good Egg.
top_picks.why_it_fits: Forgiveness and fairness in friendships — social repair, not basic emotion naming.
top_picks.caveats: Some wordplay lands above toddler level; the visual story carries it.
top_picks.gift_note: Funny enough to re-read after a squabble.
top_picks.ownership_note: Food Group series is widely owned — borrow before buying another.
